text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY CONSULTATION NOTE
Multidisciplinary Tumor Board Review and Initial Medical Oncology Consult

---

ATTENDING: [Attending Thoracic Oncologist]
CONSULTING SERVICE: Medical Oncology / Thoracic Oncology
REFERRING PROVIDER: Primary Care Medicine
Reason for Consult: Newly diagnosed stage IIIB lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant. Multidisciplinary management planning.

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Margaret Tran is a 62-year-old Vietnamese-American woman who presents to the thoracic oncology clinic for initial consultation following recent diagnosis of right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, stage IIIB (T3N2M0), with EGFR L858R mutation confirmed on tissue NGS.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Tran is a 62-year-old woman with a history of well-controlled hypertension, hypothyroidism, and a remote 12 pack-year smoking history (quit approximately 18 years ago) who was referred to her PCP approximately 6-8 weeks ago with progressive exertional dyspnea, mild right-sided chest discomfort, and approximately 4 kg unintentional weight loss over that same period. She initially attributed her symptoms to seasonal allergies and occupational stress as a high school biology teacher, but the persistence and progression of her dyspnea prompted evaluation.

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed at that time demonstrated a 4.8 cm spiculated right lower lobe mass with ipsilateral mediastinal and subcarinal lymphadenopathy (stations 4R and 7). No pleural effusion. CT abdomen/pelvis showed no hepatic, adrenal, or intraabdominal abnormalities at that time. PET/CT subsequently confirmed an FDG-avid right lower lobe mass (SUVmax 14.2) and hypermetabolic mediastinal nodes (station 4R SUVmax 9.8, subcarinal SUVmax 11.3) with no distant FDG-avid lesions. MRI brain with and without contrast showed no evidence of intracranial metastatic disease.

CT-guided transthoracic needle biopsy of the right lower lobe mass confirmed moderately differentiated lung adenocarcinoma with acinar and lepidic growth patterns. IHC: TTF-1 positive, Napsin A positive, CK7 positive, CK20 negative, p40 negative. PD-L1 TPS by 22C3 assay: 15%. Tissue NGS (523-gene panel, tumor-normal paired analysis) returned and is notable for: EGFR p.L858R exon 21 activating mutation (allele frequency 34.2%, tier 1 actionable); TP53 p.R175H co-mutation (allele frequency 41.8%); STK11 wild type; KEAP1 wild type; ALK/ROS1/RET fusions: all negative; KRAS: wild type; MET copy number 2.1 (no amplification); TMB: 6.2 mutations/Mb (low); MSS. No germline pathogenic variants identified.

The case was reviewed at our multidisciplinary thoracic oncology tumor board today with representation from thoracic surgery, radiation oncology, and medical oncology.

Thoracic Surgery Assessment: Given the presence of bulky ipsilateral mediastinal adenopathy at stations 4R and 7, Ms. Tran is not a surgical candidate at this time. Upfront surgical resection is not recommended for stage IIIB N2 disease.

Radiation Oncology Assessment: Concurrent chemoradiation (chemo-RT) per PACIFIC trial paradigm with subsequent Imfinzi consolidation was discussed. However, the team noted that the patient's EGFR mutation status significantly alters this calculus — Imfinzi consolidation post-chemo-RT is of uncertain benefit (and may be inferior) in EGFR-mutant NSCLC based on retrospective subset analyses of PACIFIC. The emerging LAURA trial data supporting osi maintenance after definitive CRT was reviewed in detail.

Medical Oncology Assessment: Given the actionable EGFR L858R mutation, osi (Tagrisso) is the preferred systemic therapy approach. In the context of stage IIIB unresectable disease with EGFR mutation, the following options were discussed with the patient:
  (a) Definitive concurrent chemoradiation followed by osi maintenance (LAURA trial schema)
  (b) Upfront osi as first-line systemic therapy, deferring radiation

The patient was counseled extensively regarding both options, including expected response rates, toxicity profiles, and the anticipated development of acquired resistance. She expressed a strong preference to avoid the acute and cumulative toxicities of thoracic radiation and platinum-based chemotherapy given her desire to continue teaching. After thorough informed discussion, the decision was made to initiate Tagrisso 80 mg orally daily as first-line targeted therapy. The patient understands that this is an individualized decision and that monitoring for resistance is essential.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Positive: Exertional dyspnea (improved slightly since initial presentation), mild right-sided chest discomfort, 4 kg weight loss over 6-8 weeks, mild fatigue.
Negative: No hemoptysis, no cough productive of sputum, no fevers or chills, no night sweats, no neurological symptoms (no headache, no visual changes, no focal weakness or numbness), no bone pain, no abdominal pain, no nausea or vomiting, no change in bowel habits.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY

1. Hypertension — well-controlled, diagnosed approximately 8 years ago
2. Hypothyroidism — on stable levothyroxine replacement, last TSH within normal limits
3. No prior history of malignancy
4. No prior thoracic surgeries or procedures

SURGICAL HISTORY

- Appendectomy (remote, childhood)
- No other significant surgical procedures

SOCIAL HISTORY

Ms. Tran was born in Saigon, Vietnam, and immigrated to the United States at age 14. She is currently employed as a high school biology teacher and describes her work as highly meaningful. She lives with her adult daughter. She is a former smoker — 12 pack-year history, quit approximately 18 years ago. She does not use alcohol or illicit substances. She is not aware of any significant occupational exposures beyond general classroom settings. She reports limited English was a concern initially but communicates very fluently; interpreter services offered and declined.

FAMILY HISTORY

- Father: deceased, lung cancer (diagnosis at age 70, heavy smoker)
- Mother: deceased, cardiovascular disease
- No known family history of other malignancies; no known hereditary cancer syndromes
- No family history of EGFR-mutant lung cancer or other targetable driver mutations discussed with the patient

ALLERGIES

- No known drug allergies
- No known food or environmental allergies

MEDICATIONS

1. Levothyroxine 88 mcg orally daily (hypothyroidism)
2. Amlodipine 5 mg orally daily (hypertension)
3. Calcium + vitamin D supplement daily (patient-initiated)
4. No anticoagulants, NSAIDs, or immunosuppressants

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital Signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 78 bpm, RR 16/min, SpO2 96% on room air, Temp 36.8°C, Weight 58 kg, Height 157 cm, BSA ~1.62 m2

General: Alert and oriented, well-appearing, mildly anxious but cooperative, conversing in English fluently. No acute distress.

HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpated.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops.

Pulmonary: Decreased breath sounds at the right base. No wheezing or stridor. Mild dullness to percussion at right base. No egophony.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly palpated. Bowel sounds active.

Extremities: No peripheral edema. No clubbing or cyanosis. Peripheral pulses intact.

Neurological: Alert and oriented x3. CN II-XII grossly intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Gait steady.

Skin: No rash. No suspicious skin lesions.

Lymph Nodes: No axillary, inguinal, or palpable supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

---

LAB RESULTS

CBC: WBC 7.2 x10^9/L, Hgb 12.4 g/dL, Plt 234 x10^9/L — mild normocytic anemia consistent with chronic disease
CMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cr 0.82, BUN 14, Glucose 94; all within normal limits
LFTs: AST 22, ALT 18, Alk Phos 78, total bilirubin 0.6 — all within normal limits
Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (stable, on current levothyroxine dose)
EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, QTc 432 ms (within normal range)

---

IMAGING REVIEW

CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with contrast: 4.8 cm spiculated right lower lobe mass with ipsilateral mediastinal and subcarinal lymphadenopathy (stations 4R and 7). No pleural effusion. No hepatic or adrenal lesions at baseline.

PET/CT: FDG-avid right lower lobe primary (SUVmax 14.2). Metabolically active mediastinal nodes as above. No evidence of distant metabolic disease outside the thorax.

MRI Brain: No intracranial metastases. No leptomeningeal enhancement.

Pathology/NGS summary as above.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Tran is a 62-year-old former smoker with newly diagnosed stage IIIB (T3N2M0) right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 21 L858R activating mutation, TTF-1/Napsin A positive, PD-L1 TPS 15%, TMB-low (6.2 mut/Mb), co-mutant TP53 R175H. She is not a surgical candidate given N2 mediastinal involvement. After multidisciplinary tumor board review and extensive patient counseling, she and her family have elected to proceed with upfront osi (Tagrisso) targeted therapy rather than concurrent chemoradiation.

1. Initiate Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally once daily as first-line systemic therapy for stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC.
   - Rationale: Third-generation EGFR TKI; superior CNS penetration; FLAURA data support osi over first/second-generation TKIs; preferred for EGFR-mutant NSCLC regardless of exon 19 del vs L858R subtype.
   - Counseled regarding expected side effects: acneiform/papulopustular rash, paronychia, diarrhea, dry skin, possible QTc prolongation, rare ILD (~3%); patient instructed to contact clinic promptly for new respiratory symptoms.
   - ECOG PS 1 at initiation.

2. Baseline and monitoring:
   - EKG at baseline (QTc 432 ms — acceptable, will recheck at 4 weeks).
   - Thyroid function monitoring given risk of osi-associated thyroid changes and baseline hypothyroidism.
   - CBC, CMP, LFTs at baseline and with each follow-up visit.
   - Ophthalmology referral deferred unless visual symptoms develop.

3. Radiation oncology: Thoracic radiation deferred at this time per patient preference and multidisciplinary discussion. Radiation oncology will remain involved for potential future need or if systemic control proves insufficient.

4. Restaging imaging planned at approximately 3 months (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis; MRI brain if clinically indicated).

5. Patient education and support:
   - Provided written materials regarding lung cancer and Tagrisso therapy in English.
   - Interpreter services offered, declined.
   - Referred to lung cancer nurse navigator for ongoing support, symptom management education, and coordination.
   - Advised on skin care regimen prophylactically (gentle cleanser, fragrance-free moisturizer, sunscreen) ahead of anticipated EGFR TKI-related dermatologic side effects.
   - Smoking cessation re-counseling documented (patient already a non-smoker x 18 years).

6. Cancer present. No prior systemic therapy. Plan is to reassess response and tolerance at first follow-up in 4-6 weeks with laboratory evaluation, then formal restaging CT at 3 months.

Electronically signed by: [Thoracic Oncology Attending Physician]
Dictated and reviewed by: [Fellow/NP co-signer]
